mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS across consecutive monthly readings signals an active tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose from near-zero to 0.33 percent by April 2022 marking start of hiking cycle
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window from a cyclical low, it marks an inflection in the labor market that historically precedes broader growth deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over trailing 3 months from a cyclical low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6 percent near multi-decade lows as of April 2022
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rejected if in more than 50 percent of historical instances of UNRATE rising 0.2pp+ in 3 months, subsequent 60-day SPX returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of declining HOUST alongside rising FEDFUNDS indicates that monetary tightening is transmitting into interest-rate-sensitive housing demand, an early real-economy stress signal that leads broader equity weakness over the next 20 trading days, producing negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1793k units in March 2022 while FEDFUNDS rate hikes were beginning
failure_condition: Hypothesis fails if joint HOUST-down/FEDFUNDS-up months show no statistically significant negative 20-day forward return relative to unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across at least two tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, which propagates into short-horizon S&P 500 gains over the next 5 trading days via positive earnings-revision sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5853 in March 2022 continuing post-pandemic recovery trend
failure_condition: Fails if Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of high CPIAUCSL inflation combined with rising FEDFUNDS, equity markets price in aggressive future tightening, producing negative 60-day forward returns; however, if CPIAUCSL growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS continues rising, the same rate path is reinterpreted as disinflationary progress and the effect reverses to positive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 6 percent while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 288.663 in April 2022 with year-over-year inflation near multi-decade highs
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with high inflation
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly more negative during high-CPI tightening periods than during low-CPI tightening periods (difference-in-means p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month growth in PAYEMS signals resilient labor demand and household income support for consumption, which sustains corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151314 thousand persons in April 2022 reflecting continued payroll expansion
failure_condition: Fails if positive PAYEMS growth months do not show significantly higher mean 20-day forward SPX returns than negative PAYEMS growth months (p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS exceeding 0.3 percent signals an abrupt weakening in consumer spending that front-runs downward revisions to GDP and corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 667724 million dollars in March 2022 amid rising inflation pressure on real consumption
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered RSAFS decline events show 5-day forward SPX returns that are positive in more than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE sits below 4 percent (indicating a tight labor market) while FEDFUNDS is rising at an accelerating pace, markets price rising risk of monetary overtightening intended to cool wage growth, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as recession probability is repriced upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4 percent and FEDFUNDS increases in at least two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6 percent in April 2022 alongside rapid FEDFUNDS increases beginning in 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns conditional on this joint trigger are not significantly more negative than the unconditional historical mean (p > 0.05) across at least two tightening episodes.
